subject: PORT TO MASTER: MPI-246 auto-mask fix (Ultralytics path-heal). Already fixed in dev 1.2.0; master 1.1.0 still broken for Pro subscribers — bump to 1.1.1.
body:
## Why this lands on your plate

You're addressing the 1.1.0-release defects on master. This is one of them: **auto-mask is broken on the Windows-local engine in the released 1.1.0 build.** Pro/Patreon subscribers on 1.1.0 cannot use Mask -> Detect. The user wants master patched and bumped to **1.1.1**.

The fix is already committed on dev branch **1.2.0** (commit `aa958869`). It has NOT been ported to master. I verified master's `comfyController.js` still carries the unpatched heal block. Carry the SAME fix to master.

---

## The bug

Auto-mask (Mask tool -> Detect) fails on Windows-local with:

```
UltralyticsDetectorProvider 1587: Value not in list: model_name: 'bbox\face_yolov8n.pt' not in ['bbox/face_yolov8n.pt', ...]
```

**Root cause:** `comfyController.runWorkflow` heals path separators over a blanket `PATH_INPUTS` list that includes `model_name`. MPI-229 added a Windows-local INVERSE heal (`/` -> `\`) so a LoRA name persisted during a Linux Pod session matches the Windows loader enum. Correct for `folder_paths`-backed loaders (they list with the OS separator). WRONG for `UltralyticsDetectorProvider`: Impact Pack builds its `model_name` list itself and ALWAYS uses forward slashes on every OS. So the Windows `/`->`\` heal corrupts `bbox/face_yolov8n.pt` -> `bbox\face_yolov8n.pt` -> value_not_in_list.

Verified live via /object_info on a Windows-local box:
- LoraLoader.lora_name -> 'CHROMA\\Amira-...safetensors' (backslash, folder_paths)
- UltralyticsDetectorProvider.model_name -> 'bbox/face_yolov8n.pt' (forward slash, self-listed)

**Scope:** Windows-local ONLY. Linux/mac-local + remote use the `\`->`/` branch, which leaves an already-forward-slash value untouched. Confirmed both directions.

**NOT a Pod-image change** — pure app-side JS. Does not affect the MPI-244 rebuild and the rebuild does not affect it.

---

## The fix (exact edit)

In `js/services/comfyController.js`, in the PATH_INPUTS heal loop (on master ~L1164-1180, right after `const _healToSlash = _needsPathHeal(this._alwaysLocal);`):

1. Add a slash-only-node exclusion set before the loop:

```js
// MPI-246: some nodes build their own enum with hardcoded '/' regardless of
// OS -- Impact Pack's UltralyticsDetectorProvider lists 'bbox/face_yolov8n.pt'
// with a forward slash on Windows too. The Windows-local '/'->'\\' heal below
// corrupts that into 'bbox\\face_yolov8n.pt' -> value_not_in_list. Exclude
// model_name for these node types (harmless in the '\\'->'/' direction too,
// their value never has backslashes). folder_paths-backed loaders
// (UpscaleModelLoader, SAMLoader, etc.) still get the OS-separator heal.
const SLASH_ONLY_NODE_TYPES = new Set(['UltralyticsDetectorProvider']);
```

2. As the FIRST line inside `for (const k of PATH_INPUTS) {`, add:

```js
if (k === 'model_name' && SLASH_ONLY_NODE_TYPES.has(node.class_type)) continue;
```

That's the whole fix (9 lines). `class_type` is the API-prompt node field. Do NOT delete `model_name` from PATH_INPUTS — UpscaleModelLoader's `model_name` DOES need the OS-separator heal; keying on class_type is the correct scope.

### Standalone self-check (passes on my branch)
- Windows-local ('/'->'\\'): UltralyticsDetectorProvider.model_name 'bbox/face_yolov8n.pt' stays UNCHANGED; UpscaleModelLoader.model_name 'sub/4x.pth' -> 'sub\\4x.pth'; LoraLoader.lora_name still heals.
- Linux/remote ('\\'->'/'): Ultralytics value untouched (already '/'); lora still heals.

---

## What the user wants you to do with it (patch flow)

Apply the fix on **master**, then run the **mpi-apply-patch** flow: bump 3rd digit 1.1.0 -> **1.1.1** (appVersion.js + package.json + package-lock.json), releaseNotes.js block + archival docs/releases md, fold the relevant UNRELEASED item, copy-review gate, rebuild via CI (ref=master), refresh the EXISTING minor Cloudflare link (no new link, no GC), then carry the same fix onto the current dev branch WITHOUT bumping it. All live ops (push/build/R2) STOP for user authorization.

On dev branch 1.2.0 the fix is already in (aa958869) so the 'carry to dev' step is DONE for 1.2.0 — just confirm and add the UNRELEASED note if missing. I did NOT run any patch/bump step; master is untouched (I only read it).

Cross-ref: MPI-198 (same heal block, opposite direction) already has a note that this exclusion is orthogonal and does not regress its Linux/mac baked-LoRA fix.

provenance.verified_by: read master:js/services/comfyController.js at 2026-07-10 -- heal block still unpatched
